title: alchemilla vulgaris subvar. obtusa
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. obtusa, also known as Lady's Mantle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to Europe. Traditionally, Lady's Mantle has been used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Alchemilla vulgaris subvar. obtusa include its ability to alleviate menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and other symptoms associated with PMS, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and promote wound healing. Lady's Mantle can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, although its effectiveness and safety should be evaluated by a healthcare professional before use.
